behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is parser output handed to hook-input handling, not just JSON.parse in isolation. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text does not require real process/stdin/OS; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI step is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser + normal hook input handler with an in-process trace/handler sink. Feed valid JSON where string fields include code fences, backticks, and $vars. Assert parse succeeds and downstream handler path executes (no parse-failure trace, normal handling signal present).

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is specifically parser error -> trace record content, a module handoff. REAL-INFRA: not required if malformed bytes are supplied directly in-process. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text asks for trace semantics, not process/OS behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run parser and trace emitter together with malformed JSON inputs. Assert `json_parse_failed` is emitted and includes diagnostic context fields (e.g., offset/snippet/error message) sufficient to localize malformed section.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is about mapping parser failure into policy outcome plus telemetry intent, typically crossing policy + telemetry modules. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior explicitly says local path; Agentic/Workflow rejected due no model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a parse-failure object through the real local error-policy path with telemetry marker wiring enabled. Assert returned result is handled (no exception) and telemetry intent flag/event is set.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Could be Unit if mapping is a single pure function, but behavior wording suggests policy-to-telemetry wiring.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit real process execution and malformed stdin/exit behavior require subprocess + OS I/O semantics. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can miss crash/exit/pipe timing issues. Rejection gate: Integration rejected by behavior text "In real process execution".
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the hook as a real subprocess, pipe malformed stdin, wait for exit, and capture emitted trace output/artifact. Assert process does not crash unexpectedly and `json_parse_failed` signal is emitted before termination.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires real invocation via stdin path, so subprocess/OS I/O boundary is part of failure mode. MOCK-HIDE: mocks can hide boundary bugs in stream handling and artifact emission timing. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because text says "real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)".
behaviors.test_description: Execute the hook through its real entrypoint with malformed stdin and configured trace sink. Verify the expected trace artifact is produced and contains parse-failure classification.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: distinction depends on end-to-end classification across parser + failure reporting path, not just one parser call. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejection gate: System rejected because no requirement for real OS/subprocess; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model output quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Create paired regression cases in one harness: (a) valid JSON with markdown-heavy strings, (b) malformed JSON. Run through parser + classification/trace pipeline and assert only (b) yields `json_parse_failed`, while (a) follows normal path.
